blocks.original_text: Her father had been fighting with the council again. Arya could see it on his face when he came to table, late again, as he had been so often. The first course, a thick sweet soup made with pumpkins, had already been taken away when Ned Stark strode into the Small Hall. They called it that to set it apart from the Great Hall, where the king could feast a thousand, but it was a long room with a high vaulted ceiling and bench space for two hundred at its trestle tables.

A hand of beaten silver clutched the woolen folds of each cloak and marked their wearers as men of the Hand’s household guard. There were only fifty of them, so most of the benches were empty.

blocks.original_text: No one talked to Arya. She didn’t care. She liked it that way. She would have eaten her meals alone in her bedchamber if they let her. Sometimes they did, when Father had to dine with the king or some lord or the envoys from this place or that place. The rest of the time, they ate in his solar, just him and her and Sansa. That was when Arya missed her brothers most. She wanted to tease Bran and play with baby Rickon and have Robb smile at her. She wanted Jon to muss up her hair and call her “little sister” and finish her sentences with her. But all of them were gone. She had no one left but Sansa, and Sansa wouldn’t even talk to her unless Father made her.
blocks.explanation_zh: 无人和Arya说话；她思念所有兄弟，尤其能叫她little sister并接话的Jon。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Arya口称喜欢无人说话，随后长段思念家人；孤独盔甲与真实渴望并存。

blocks.original_text: Back at Winterfell, they had eaten in the Great Hall almost half the time. Her father used to say that a lord needed to eat with his men, if he hoped to keep them. “Know the men who follow you,” she heard him tell Robb once, “and let them know you. Don’t ask your men to die for a stranger.” At Winterfell, he always had an extra seat set at his own table, and every day a different man would be asked to join him. One night it would be Vayon Poole, and the talk would be coppers and bread stores and servants. The next time it would be Mikken, and her father would listen to him go on about armor and swords and how hot a forge should be and the best way to temper steel. Another day it might be Hullen with his endless horse talk, or Septon Chayle from the library, or Jory, or Ser Rodrik, or even Old Nan with her stories.
blocks.explanation_zh: 她回忆Winterfell时Eddard每天邀不同下属同桌，教Robb认识追随者、不要让人为陌生人死。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Eddard的领导原则不是抽象亲民，而是把财政、锻造、马匹、宗教和故事带到同一张桌上。
blocks.background_zh: **北方治理：** Eddard在Winterfell轮流邀请steward、smith、master of horse、septon与guards同桌，以维持个人关系和信息流。

blocks.original_text: Arya had loved nothing better than to sit at her father’s table and listen to them talk. She had loved listening to the men on the benches too; to freeriders tough as leather, courtly knights and bold young squires, grizzled old men-at-arms. She used to throw snowballs at them and help them steal pies from the kitchen. Their wives gave her scones and she invented names for their babies and played monsters-and-maidens and hide-the-treasure and come-into-my-castle with their children. Fat Tom used to call her “Arya Underfoot,” because he said that was where she always was. She’d liked that a lot better than “Arya Horseface.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Arya过去爱听各阶层人员说话、与家属玩耍，喜欢Arya Underfoot胜过Horseface。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Underfoot原可被视为烦人，Arya却因它证明自己属于人群而喜欢；Horseface只剩外貌羞辱。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在Arya的grief、自责、家庭归属、Eddard的教育选择或Syrio训练方法。

blocks.original_text: Only that was Winterfell, a world away, and now everything was changed. This was the first time they had supped with the men since arriving in King’s Landing. Arya hated it. She hated the sounds of their voices now, the way they laughed, the stories they told. They’d been her friends, she’d felt safe around them, but now she knew that was a lie. They’d let the queen kill Lady, that was horrible enough, but then the Hound found Mycah. Jeyne Poole had told Arya that he’d cut him up in so many pieces that they’d given him back to the butcher in a bag, and at first the poor man had thought it was a pig they’d slaughtered. And no one had raised a voice or drawn a blade or anything, not Harwin who always talked so bold, or Alyn who was going to be a knight, or Jory who was captain of the guard. Not even her father.
blocks.explanation_zh: King’s Landing的创伤改变安全感；她认为guards任由Lady和Mycah被杀，连Eddard也没有拔剑。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Arya说安全是lie，不是guards突然改变，而是她发现他们的忠诚受命令和权力边界限制。
blocks.background_zh: **文本校正：** Jeyne关于Mycah被切成许多块、装袋误认猪的说法是转述夸张；Eddard实际看到的是几乎被一刀从肩劈至腰。

blocks.original_text: Arya went to the chest at the foot of her bed.
